title: Index Pairs of a String
description:
Given a string text and an array of strings words, return _an array of all index pairs_ [i, j] _so that the substring_ text[i...j] _is in words_.

Return the pairs [i, j] in sorted order (i.e., sort them by their first coordinate, and in case of ties sort them by their second coordinate).

solutionCode.python:
```python
def has_all_codes_in_range(s: str, n: int) -> bool:
    substrings = set()
    length = len(bin(n)) - 2
    for i in range(len(s) - length + 1):
        substrings.add(s[i:i + length])
    return len(substrings) == n
```

    
    1. Initialize an empty set called `substrings` to store unique binary substrings of length equal to the binary representation of `n`.
2. Compute the length of the binary representation of `n` and store it in `length`.
3. Loop through the binary string `s`, starting from index 0 to `len(s) - length`:
   a. Extract the substring of `s` with length `length` starting at index `i`.
   b. Add the extracted substring to the `substrings` set.
4. Check if the size of `substrings` is equal to `n`. If it is, then all integers in the range `[1, n]` had their binary representation in `s`.
5. Return the result (true if the size is equal to `n`, and false otherwise).
